old_claim.note: Textile Dye Guilds provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The putting-out system is a domestic-production system organized around household out-workers doing textile work for clothiers or merchants.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract says the putting-out or domestic system was a key feature of England's woollen cloth industry and examines late-medieval clothiers and their dependent out-workers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source defines the system through domestic textile production and dependent out-workers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Household textile production is the domestic production base of the putting-out system; textile dye guilds are a narrower finishing/regulation institution and not the core method.

ontology_before: Putting-Out Textile System depended on Textile Dye Guilds, conflating a distributed domestic production system with specialist dye-guild regulation.
ontology_after: Putting-Out Textile System depends on Household Textile Production for the domestic out-worker production base.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: putting_out_textile_system now depends on household_textile_production and no longer directly depends on textile_dye_guilds.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to dyeing and finishing guild regulation rather than putting-out production.
A stronger source showed putting-out textile production could exist without household or domestic textile work.
The household_textile_production node were narrowed away from spinning, weaving, dyeing, or textile repair work.
short_reason: Putting-out textile production is domestic household out-work, not dye-guild regulation.
